description.content: Ocean shipping booking management
Returns a list of ocean shipping bookings. Supports filtering by carrier, booking date, port of origin, port of destination, and booking status.

query.description: Filter bookings created after this date (ISO 8601)

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Submits a new ocean booking request to the specified carrier through the INTTRA network. Returns a booking confirmation with carrier booking reference.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Returns full details for a specific ocean booking.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Modifies an existing ocean booking. Subject to carrier acceptance.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Cancels an ocean booking. Subject to carrier cancellation policies.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Ocean carrier schedule lookup
Returns vessel sailing schedules from origin to destination port. Supports filtering by carrier, vessel, and date range.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Bill of lading shipping instructions
Submits shipping instructions (bill of lading instructions) to the carrier for a confirmed booking. Includes cargo description, consignee, notify parties, and special instructions.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Container and shipment tracking
Returns current location, status, and event history for a specific container. Provides real-time tracking from all INTTRA carrier partners.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Returns tracking events for all containers in a booking.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

INTTRA (now part of e2open) provides ocean execution APIs for the world's largest multi-carrier e-commerce platform for global shipping. The RESTful API uses HTTPS with JSON for booking, ocean schedules, rates, and visibility/track and trace products. Authentication requires a bearer token obtained from the identity service before making API requests.
